content:
Case 7:
Initial presentation:
- Age: 70
- Sex: male
- Chief complaint: New nodular lesion on the left ankle with swelling and discoloration
- Vital signs: BP 128/76 mmHg, HR 82/min (regular), T° 36.8 °C, RR 16/min, SpO₂ 96% on room air.
Setting: Outpatient clinic (initial presentation with localized nodular lesion)
Capabilities: You are evaluating a clinically stable patient in an ambulatory setting. You can obtain a focused history and examination, request standard laboratory tests, and arrange initial non-invasive imaging appropriate for a first diagnostic assessment. Invasive tissue sampling and more complex diagnostic procedures usually require additional planning.
Provide either a request for a single information item or solve.

History Physical:
January 15, 2025: The patient presented to the clinic with a newly developed nodular lesion on the left ankle, measuring approximately 3-4 cm in diameter, associated with swelling and dark brown discoloration. He reported mild localized pain at the site but was otherwise in good general condition. The patient described intermittent low-grade fevers at night but denied night sweats or significant weight loss. Chronic headaches were noted, unchanged in character or intensity over several years.
Physical examination: Single firm, slightly tender, nodular lesion (3-4 cm) on the left ankle with dark brown pigmentation and mild surrounding edema. No additional cutaneous lesions were observed. No palpable lymphadenopathy. Abdomen soft and non-tender, no hepatosplenomegaly. Lungs clear on auscultation, heart sounds regular, no peripheral edema.
Neurological examination: Patient alert and fully oriented. Cranial nerves II–XII intact: pupils equal and reactive, extraocular movements full, visual fields intact, facial sensation and symmetry normal, palate elevation symmetrical, tongue midline, and hearing preserved. Motor exam: Normal tone and bulk, 5/5 strength in all extremities. Reflexes: 2+ and symmetric. Sensation: Intact to light touch, pinprick, vibration, and proprioception throughout. Coordination: Normal finger-to-nose and heel-to-shin testing.
Current medication: Amlodipine 5 mg 1-0-0, bisoprolol 2.5 mg 1-0-1, atorvastatin 20 mg 0-0-1, pantoprazole 40 mg 1-0-0, vitamin D 20,000 IU weekly, valaciclovir 500 mg 1-0-1, co-trimoxazole 960 mg 1-0-0 (Mon/Wed/Fri), metformin 1000 mg 1-0-0, apixaban 5 mg 1-0-1.

'history_physical' already provided.

Demographics:
Age: 70 years
Sex: Male
Performance status: ECOG 0-1
Comorbidities: Arterial hypertension, type 2 diabetes mellitus, aortic stenosis, tachyarrhythmia with episodes of atrial flutter, and a history of acute renal failure during a prior septic episode.
Social: Married, lives alone, three adult children. Former smoker (35 pack-years). Denies current alcohol or illicit drug use.
Family history: Mother deceased (lung cancer), father deceased (natural causes).
Height: 181 cm; Weight: 102 kg

Cbc:
CBC: Hb 10.7 g/dL, WBC 2.7 ×10⁹/L, ANC 1.7 ×10⁹/L, Plt 103 ×10⁹/L; MCV normal, MCH normal; differential: 63% neutrophils, 10% monocytes, 25% lymphocytes, 2% nuclear debris.

Chemistry:
Na 126 mmol/L, K 4.1 mmol/L, creatinine 1.2 mg/dL, BUN 23 mg/dL, AST 76 U/L, ALT 86 U/L, bilirubin 1.3 mg/dL, LDH 260 U/L, uric acid 7.2 mg/dL, Ca²⁺ 2.55 mmol/L, phosphate 3.4 mg/dL, lactate 1.3 mmol/L, CK 89 U/L, Troponin T 0.012 ng/mL, NT-proBNP 235 pg/mL.

Abdominal Ultrasound:
January 15, 2025 – Abdominal ultrasound: Normal-sized liver and spleen, no hepatosplenomegaly, no retroperitoneal or mesenteric lymphadenopathy.

'CT chest' not available. Try 'pet_ct' instead.
This case does not list a dedicated chest CT. A related imaging item available in later rounds is PET-CT, which provides thoracic imaging as part of whole-body assessment. You can request available current-round data such as history/physical or basic labs instead.

Histology/biopsy results for the lesion are represented by the soft_tissue_biopsy item, but that information is not released until round 2. In this round you can request history/physical, labs (CBC, chemistry, coagulation), or abdominal ultrasound or echocardiography instead.
(3 invalid requests remaining before case is cancelled. Consider solving with available information or requesting a different item.)

Echocardiography:
Echocardiogram: Normal left ventricular systolic function (LVEF 53%), mild aortic stenosis (grade I/III), atrial fibrillation, no pericardial effusion.

content:
Round 2 begins.
Setting: Inpatient or day-hospital workup (histologic and staging evaluation)
Capabilities: The patient is now undergoing further diagnostic clarification. You can organize tissue sampling, detailed histopathology and immunophenotyping, comprehensive staging procedures, and extended evaluations by consulting services. Some specialized results may still be in progress.
Request additional information or solve.

Soft Tissue Biopsy:
Core biopsy from the soft tissue mass of the left ankle revealed a diffuse, dense infiltrate of atypical lymphoid cells, completely effacing the normal tissue architecture. The infiltrate was composed of large lymphoid cells with round to oval nuclei, vesicular chromatin, and prominent nucleoli, exhibiting moderate basophilic cytoplasm, frequent mitoses, and scattered apoptotic bodies. No follicular, nodular, or germinal center pattern was identified.
Immunohistochemistry:
Tumor cells showed strong diffuse positivity for CD19, CD79a, and PAX5, but were negative for CD20, consistent with CD20-negative high-grade B-cell lymphoma. Cells were positive for CD10 and BCL6, but negative for MUM1/IRF4, CD3, CD5, CD30, CD15, and cyclin D1. BCL2 was positive, and MYC was expressed in approximately 50% of tumor cells. Ki-67 proliferation index was around 80%.
EBER in situ hybridization was negative.

Pet Ct:
January 21, 2025: FDG PET-CT: Demonstrated a solitary, intensely FDG-avid soft-tissue lesion at the left ankle (SUVmax 11.4; Deauville score 5), correlating with the clinically visible nodule. No lymphadenopathy, bone marrow uptake, or visceral involvement. Mild calcification of the aortic valve leaflets was incidentally noted, consistent with early aortic sclerosis. Mild centrilobular pulmonary emphysema was also present.
Lumbar puncture and CSF analysis: Clear cerebrospinal fluid; cytology and flow cytometry negative for malignant lymphoid cells.

Cytogenetics:
FISH: IGH::MYC and IGH::BCL2 rearrangements

solve.working_diagnosis: High-grade B-cell lymphoma with MYC and BCL2 rearrangements (Double-Hit Lymphoma), CD20-negative, Stage IE

treatment_plan.action: DA-EPOCH chemotherapy (omit Rituximab due to CD20 negativity)
Involved Site Radiotherapy (ISRT) to left ankle consolidation
Tumor Lysis Syndrome prophylaxis (fluids, uric acid lowering agents)

content:
Round 3 begins.
Setting: Specialist conference (definitive diagnosis and treatment planning)
Capabilities: You now have access to definitive pathology, molecular findings, prior treatment history, and full staging results. You are expected to integrate these data with comorbidity and transplant scores to establish the final diagnosis, characterize risk, and propose an evidence-based treatment and follow-up strategy.
Request additional information or solve.
Final round - provide definitive diagnosis and treatment plan.
